description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof clad in light green shingles, with two prominent dormers adding architectural interest. The exterior walls are finished in orange-red brick, and the front facade showcases a large bay window with pointed arches, reminiscent of Gothic Revival influences. A covered porch with white railings and posts extends from the right side of the front, providing a welcoming entryway. The windows are framed in white, with a mix of rectangular and pointed shapes, and the roof is topped with small finials. The structure appears to be two stories tall, with a symmetrical yet visually dynamic arrangement of rooflines and window placements. The overall design suggests a blend of Victorian and Gothic Revival architectural styles, making it a distinctive residential building in an urban neighborhood.
description: A two-story brick house with a steep green gable roof, pointed dormers, a large arched bay window, and a covered front porch with white railings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, large arched bay window, and two dormers on the steep green roof.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the triangular gable end, two rectangular windows, and the side of the porch.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain brick wall with no windows or doors, and the full expanse of the green shingled roof.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the gable end and the side of the bay window, with no visible entry or porch.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large arched bay window and dormer windows
attribute_entryway_design: Covered porch with white railings and posts, steps leading to a single main door, and decorative trim
